acceptance_criteria: `PolyphonyTag` DU exists with `State`, `Intent`, `Facet` variants and a `TryParse` entry point
`AllOwned(TagSet)` enumerates every polyphony-owned tag present on a tag set
Existing `PolyphonyTags` string constants are the single source of raw values consumed by the DU (no duplication)
Unit tests cover round-trip parse, unknown-tag handling, and `AllOwned` enumeration
Build passes with zero errors and warnings; all existing tests pass
description:
Add a typed `PolyphonyTag` discriminated union under `src/Polyphony/Tagging/` with three minimal variants — `State` (e.g. `Planned`), `Intent` (`Root`, `InScope`), `Facet` (`polyphony:facets=<csv>` parsed via the existing `FacetTagParser`). Provide `TryParse(string raw)` and an `AllOwned(TagSet)` enumerator that yields every polyphony-owned tag present on a tag set. The existing string constants in [src/Polyphony/Tagging/PolyphonyTags.cs](src/Polyphony/Tagging/PolyphonyTags.cs) remain the canonical raw values; the DU consumes them — do NOT duplicate the strings. Keep variants minimal: exactly what's stamped today, no speculative taxonomy.

Unit tests cover round-trip parse, unknown-tag rejection, and `AllOwned` enumeration over a mixed tag set.
title: Introduce typed PolyphonyTag DU; migrate existing constants behind it

acceptance_criteria: `ResetPlan` record enumerates: matching tags, polyphony-authored comments, state dir, local + remote branches matching all five patterns, and worktrees
Planner performs zero mutation (read-only)
`ResetPlan` serializes via `PolyphonyJsonContext` (AOT-safe)
Unit tests cover each artifact category with stubbed ADO/git boundaries
Build passes with zero errors and warnings; all existing tests pass
description:
Add `src/Polyphony/Reset/ResetPlanner.cs` (and a `ResetPlan` record) that, given a `--root-id N`, enumerates every artifact reset would touch WITHOUT performing any mutation:
- ADO tags currently set on the work item that match `PolyphonyTag.AllOwned` (from task-1)
- Polyphony-authored ADO comments (filter by author/marker consistent with how polyphony posts comments today — confirm marker by reading `PrCommands.PostCommentAdo.cs`)
- Per-root state dir `/polyphony/N/`
- Branches local + remote matching `feature/N`, `plan/N*`, `impl/N-*`, `mg/N_*`, `sdlc/apex/N`
- Worktrees under `polyphony-runs/apex-N/`

The `ResetPlan` record serializes via `PolyphonyJsonContext` and is the JSON contract the verb emits in `--dry-run` mode. Unit tests stub `IAdoClient` and the git/worktree probes; verify enumeration is complete and deterministic.
title: Implement reset planner: enumerate artifacts and emit dry-run JSON

acceptance_criteria: `polyphony reset --root-id N` exists as a CAF verb following the existing patterns
`--dry-run` emits the `ResetPlan` JSON and performs no mutation
Default invocation prompts for confirmation; `--force` skips the prompt
Comments are archived to a sidecar JSON BEFORE being cleared (verified by test)
Executor performs tag strip, comment clear, state-dir delete, branch delete (local + remote), and worktree remove
JSON output contract test added under `tests/` consistent with existing verbs
Build passes with zero errors and warnings; all existing tests pass
description:
Add `src/Polyphony/Commands/ResetCommand.cs` following the ConsoleAppFramework + `[VerbGroup]` / `[VerbResult]` / `PolyphonyJsonContext` pattern from [HealthCommand](src/Polyphony/Commands/HealthCommand.cs). Flags:
- `--root-id N` (required)
- `--dry-run` (planner-only; emit `ResetPlan` JSON, no mutation)
- `--force` (skip confirmation)

Default behaviour: run the planner, render the plan to STDERR (human-friendly), prompt for confirmation on STDIN; on confirmation, execute. The executor MUST archive polyphony-authored comments to a sidecar JSON BEFORE clearing them — sidecar location is the per-root state dir if it exists, else a sibling `polyphony-archive/N-<utc>.json`. Then perform: tag strip → comment clear → state-dir delete → branch delete (local then remote) → worktree remove. Emit a final JSON result describing what was actually mutated.

Add a JSON output contract test under `tests/` consistent with the existing `JsonOutputContractTests`. Add executor tests that verify the archive-before-clear ordering and that `--force` bypasses the prompt.
title: Implement reset executor + CLI verb (confirm/--force/--dry-run + archive sidecar)

acceptance_criteria: Verb is documented with scrub scope, flags, UX, and archive sidecar location
Documentation explicitly describes the remediation pattern that the Issue 1 refusal message will point at
Cross-references existing tag/branch docs where relevant
description: Document the new verb in `README.md` (and/or a new doc under `docs/`): scrub scope, flags, confirmation/`--force`/`--dry-run` UX, the comment-archive sidecar location and format, and the remediation pattern that the Issue 1 (#3165) refusal message will reference. Cross-link from the polyphony-cli-developer / polyphony-branch-model skill index if appropriate.
title: Document polyphony reset and the remediation pattern
